params.name: Socrates' Poison
params.source: Expert Scrolls (0.95%), Fragment Crafting (3.7%), Hellenic Treasure Maps (10%), Lottery Machine (0.08%), Wishing (0.34%)
params.description:
On Mar 29, 399 BC, the Athenian philosopher Socrates died from drinking poison hemlock. Poison hemlock is a lethal drug that causes death. Socrates was sentenced to death for blaspheming the youth of Athens. Although he had the chance to escape, he took the poison hemlock in respect of the Athen laws. On his deathbed, he told Plato to pay a neighbor for eating their chicken. [Poison hemlock] Kingdom: Plantae
Phylum: Angiosperms
Class: dicotyledon
Order: Apiales
Family: Apiaceae
